title: Analysis of a Septuple Open Cluster System and Its Extended Family in Gaia DR3
author_string: Muhammad Akmal Husain,  Ferdinand, Mochamad Ikbal Arifyanto, Muhammad Irfan Hakim
abstract: A rare multiple open cluster system has been analyzed using Gaia DR3 astrometry and photometry data. Using Agglomerative Hierarchical clustering and Bayesian-HDBSCAN, we identify a compact core consisting of seven known open clusters and two additional components, including a new candidate, forming a nine-member association. Membership probabilities are refined through statistical modeling, combining GUMM, Bayesian-KDE, and Bayesian-XDGMM for tidal tails identification. Backward orbital integrations confirm coherent motions over 20-30 million years, suggesting a common origin from the same massive molecular cloud. This system offers a unique laboratory for investigating cluster multiplicity, dynamical evolution, and Galactic structure.
